body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_15)
Regime data: VIX 17.16 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | NEUTRAL conf 51.666666666666664% | Range 729.21–769.13 | iron_condor x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime is marked by elevated volatility with VIX at 17.16, a steep HY OAS of 269 bps signaling credit stress, and a firm DXY at 101.33, while US Treasury yields remain elevated (2Y at 4.21%, 10Y at 4.56%), reflecting persistent hawkish Fed expectations. SPY is under pressure at $749.17 with a negative sentiment of -2, driven by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions that are dragging the market lower, particularly in tech-heavy Nasdaq. NVDA at $203.53 stands out with a bullish sentiment (+1) and high IV (53.24%), benefiting from the ongoing generational transfer narrative favoring semiconductor leaders, while AMZN at $247.31 faces bearish sentiment (-1) as it is perceived a loser in this shift. The near-term catalyst to watch is the trajectory of U.S.-Iran tensions, which could exacerbate risk-off flows and further suppress equities, compounded by looming inflation data that may prompt another Fed rate hike, as hinted by Fed’s Waller. The key risk is a geopolitical escalation triggering a broader market selloff and a potential hawkish Fed response, which would pressure valuations across the board. System status: trade signal gates are fully active, signaling readiness for tactical positioning.

SPY.sentiment_driver: U.S.-Iran tensions escalate, stocks fall sharply

NVDA.sentiment_driver: NVIDIA and Micron are the winners in generational transfer
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon, Google, Microsoft are the losers in generational transfer
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall after latest U.S.-Iran escalation
Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
A toxic stew drags stocks lower as we look at what can get the AI trade back on track
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, WFC, BAC, and GS reporting on July 14, followed by MS on July 15. Investors will focus on credit quality and loan growth amid a stable VIX at 17.16 and HY OAS at 269 bps, which suggest moderate risk appetite. Strong results could buoy financials and provide a tailwind for SPY, while any signs of stress could pressure the sector and weigh on broader equity sentiment.
narratives.next_week: Next week features heavyweight tech and communication services earnings, including GOOGL, TSLA, and T on July 22, with industrials and materials also reporting. GOOGL and TSLA's bullish consensus expectations contrast with the cautious tone in communication services, signaling potential sector rotation. Positive earnings surprises here could lift NVDA and AMZN via improved tech sector sentiment, while weakness may temper growth narratives and cap SPY gains.
narratives.next_month: The following month is packed with marquee earnings and key macro events, notably META and MSFT on July 29, the FOMC meeting on July 30, and inflation data on July 31. Market participants will closely watch MSFT’s bullish outlook and META’s bearish sentiment for clues on tech leadership, while the Fed’s likely hold at 3.50% and inflation readings above 3.1% could pressure growth stocks. These dynamics will be critical for positioning in SPY, NVDA, and AMZN as investors weigh policy risks against earnings momentum.

bull.trigger: JPM beats earnings and revenue estimates with strong loan growth
bull.implication: SPY rises modestly; NVDA and AMZN unaffected.

base.trigger: JPM meets earnings and revenue expectations
base.implication: SPY stable; limited impact on NVDA and AMZN.

bear.trigger: JPM misses earnings or signals credit concerns
bear.implication: SPY dips; risk-off pressure may slightly weigh on NVDA and AMZN.

base.trigger: WFC reports in line with expectations

bear.trigger: WFC misses earnings or signals loan quality issues
bear.implication: SPY declines; slight negative spillover to NVDA and AMZN.
